title: Header Fields, Representations, Negotiation, Validators, and Caching · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A server pairing session predicts request, handler, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before building the first representation selection and cache contract for header fields, representations, negotiation, validators, and caching. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of representation selection and cache contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Handle case-insensitive field names, repeated values, list syntax, hop-by-hop fields, size limits, and canonical map behavior without naïve splitting.
Validate request Content-Type and parameters, select an acceptable response representation, and return 415 or 406 with a documented default.
Set Content-Type, Content-Length strategy, Location, validators, Cache-Control, Vary, security fields, and trailers before committing response headers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Header Multivalue Canonicalization
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 2601 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to negotiate a representation and keep validator and privacy decisions independent; then cancel during the dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle case-insensitive field names, repeated values, list syntax, hop-by-hop fields, size limits, and canonical map behavior without naïve splitting.

steps.title: Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Cache Privacy Vary
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Cache Privacy Vary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop team handles case 2610 in a civic incident API behind one trusted regional proxy. Build deterministic Go evidence to negotiate a representation and keep validator and privacy decisions independent; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design shared and private caching, freshness, revalidation, no-store, stale behavior, invalidation, and Vary without exposing user-specific data.

Evidence target: Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Cache Privacy Vary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Cache Privacy Vary, keep Cache Privacy Vary connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design shared and private caching, freshness, revalidation, no-store, stale behavior, invalidation, and Vary without exposing user-specific data.

Build deterministic Go evidence to negotiate a representation and keep validator and privacy decisions independent; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design shared and private caching, freshness, revalidation, no-store, stale behavior, invalidation, and Vary without exposing user-specific data.

For Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Cache Privacy Vary, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Cache-Control private prevents browsers from storing a response.
